steps.title: Read the footsteps as a signal
steps.content: Jam knows the painting is done because of the weight and rhythm of Bitter's footsteps. The underlined sentence personifies those footsteps as communicating the news, reinforcing that Jam can understand something from the sound.

steps.title: Reject literal misreadings
steps.content: Choice A treats singing as evidence about Jam's interest in music. Choice C claims Bitter always sings while painting, which the passage never says. Choice D shifts to Aloe, who is not reacting in the underlined sentence. The answer is B.
